Title: CRA Form PE428 and Worksheet PE428 2025 Prince Edward Island resident tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5002-c/5002-c-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5002-c/5002-c-25e.pdf

- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5002-d/5002-d-25e.pdf

CRA Form PE428 2025 calculates Prince Edward Island tax for Prince Edward Island residents. Part A calculates Prince Edward Island tax on taxable income from line 26000. The bracket thresholds are $33,328, $64,656, $105,000, and $140,000. The rates are 9.5%, 13.47%, 16.6%, 17.62%, and 19%. The base tax amounts are $0.00, $3,166.16, $7,386.04, $14,083.15, and $20,250.15.

Part B calculates Prince Edward Island non-refundable tax credits. Line 58040 basic personal amount is $14,650. Worksheet PE428 line 58080 age amount has a maximum of $6,510, starts reducing above $36,600, is zero at $80,000 or more, and uses a 15% reduction rate. Lines 58120 and 58160 use a base amount of $13,687 and a maximum of $12,443 for the spouse or common-law partner amount and eligible dependant amount after subtracting the spouse or dependant net income. Line 58230 multiplies eligible young-child months by $100. Line 58360 pension income amount is capped at $1,000. Line 58440 disability amount for self is $6,890 unless the under-18 worksheet applies. Line 58500 teacher school supply amount is capped at $500. Line 58689 medical expenses are reduced by the lesser of $1,678 and 3% of net income, then medical expenses for other dependants line 58729 are added. Line 58840 multiplies the total claim amount by 9.5%. Donations and gifts line 58969 multiplies federal Schedule 9 line 13 by 9.5% and line 14 by 19%. Line 61500 adds line 58840 and line 58969 as Prince Edward Island non-refundable tax credits.

Part C calculates Prince Edward Island tax. Line 57 adds tax on taxable income and tax on split income line 61510. Line 59 subtracts non-refundable tax credits. Worksheet PE428 line 61520 calculates the dividend tax credit by multiplying taxable dividends other than eligible dividends line 12010 by 1.3% and taxable dividends line 12000 minus line 12010 by 10.5%. Line 61540 multiplies federal minimum tax carryover line 40427 by 57.5%. Line 64 multiplies Form T691 Part 7 line 14 by 57.5%. Lines 66 to 101 calculate the Prince Edward Island low-income tax reduction and unused spouse-transfer amount. The basic reduction is $350, age reductions are $250 each, spouse and eligible dependant reductions are $350 each, dependent children born in 2007 or later are $300 each, adjusted family income above $22,650 reduces the amount at 5%, and unused low-income reduction from a spouse or common-law partner is subtracted before the taxpayer's own reduction is calculated. Line 92 calculates the political contribution tax credit: contributions over $1,150 receive $500; otherwise the first $100 receives 75%, the next range to $550 receives 50% plus $75, and amounts over $550 receive 33.33% plus $300. Line 96 caps current and unused equity tax credits at $7,000. Line 97 enters Prince Edward Island tax on line 42800. Part D line 63510 gives a $1,000 refundable volunteer firefighter and volunteer search and rescue personnel tax credit entered on line 47900. Normalized numeric values encoded from PE428 and Worksheet PE428 include 33328, 64656, 105000, 140000, 0.095, 0.1347, 0.166, 0.1762, 0.19, 3166.16, 7386.04, 14083.15, 20250.15, 14650, 6510, 36600, 80000, 0.15, 13687, 12443, 100, 1000, 6890, 500, 1678, 0.03, 0.575, 0.013, 0.105, 350, 250, 300, 22650, 0.05, 550, 1150, 0.75, 0.50, 0.3333, 75, and 7000.
